specific_location: Barr Lake State Park banding station
description: The body plumage of this bird was orangish yellow with an olive tinge and the greatest amount of olive on its back. Its flight feathers were olive, and no wing bars were present. Some greater coverts were washed orange. Its uppertail coverts and crown were washed with reddish orange, and its underparts were dull yellow with the exception of its undertail coverts, which were washed bright orange. The mandible, which lacked a distinct tooth, was mostly dark gray, and the maxilla was pale, pinkish-tan. Its wing chord measured 93 mm and tail 76 mm.Behaviors: Normal behaviors of the Summer Tanager were not observed during its capture and banding.Call: not applicablePlumage: yellowish overall with olive wash
photographer: Photographer: Starr Nicely, Starr Nicely, Starr Nicely, Starr Nicely, Starr Nicely
